title: weinmannia microphylla f. humilis
intro: Weinmannia microphylla f. humilis, also known as the dwarf matipo, is a small shrub species native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Māori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The leaves of the plant are typically used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are utilized in traditional medicine and modern herbalism.
